records.ety: [F. carcasse, fr. It. carcassa, fr. L. caro flesh + capsa chest, box, case. Cf. Carnal, Case a sheath.]

defs: A dead body, whether of man or beast; a corpse; now commonly the dead body of a beast.
quotes: He turned to see the carcass of the lion.
This kept thousands in the town whose carcasses went into the great pits by cartloads.

defs: The living body; -- now commonly used in contempt or ridicule.
quotes: Lovely her face; was ne'er so fair a creature. For earthly carcass had a heavenly feature.

defs: The abandoned and decaying remains of some bulky and once comely thing, as a ship; the skeleton, or the uncovered or unfinished frame, of a thing.
quotes: A rotten carcass of a boat.

defs: A hollow case or shell, filled with combustibles, to be thrown from a mortar or howitzer, to set fire to buildings, ships, etc.
quotes: A discharge of carcasses and bombshells.
